chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.550 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.3286/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.3287/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 03.03.2025 Mr. Nasrullah Malik, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Azizullah son of Muhammad Ibrahim seeks protective bail in FIR No.8/2025, registered at P.S. SSGC, Karachi for offence under Sections 14, 15 and 25 Gas (Theft Control and Recovery) Act, 2016. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant/accused is innocent and he has been falsely implicated in this case for mala fide intentions and ulterior motives, he is a law-abiding citizen and intends to surrender before the trial Court concerned but he apprehends his arrest as the concerned police is bent upon to arrest him in order to humiliate and disgrace him, therefore, he needs some protection to enable to appear before trial Court concerned. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicant/accused Azizullah son of Muhammad Ibrahim, for a period of three days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of seven days from today or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier.

Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of seven days from today or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant/accused fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stands automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
